generated_by: Sonnet 5 (Claude Code), following the source-harvest skill's artifact contract and role-isolation rules
corpus_scope: Primary and authoritative sources bearing on the LHC micro-black-hole safety dependency question
research_question: Does the CERN safety case for excluding dangerous micro-black-hole production rest on independent, jointly sufficient physical assumptions, or on a chain with a theoretically derived (not directly experimentally confirmed) weak link?
supersedes: archive/sol-session-2026-07-19/lhc-corpus/ — that directory's .txt files and manifest are agent-written narrative summaries/paraphrases with fabricated-looking retrieval metadata (arbitrary timestamps, no verifiable HTTP status or raw bytes); they are NOT primary-source copies and must not be cited as retrieved evidence. This directory replaces them with real retrieved bytes and faithful full-text extractions.
notes_on_count: 6 sources were targeted and retrieved, covering all 5 requested categories (LSAG 2008, Giddings & Mangano 2008, the 2003 LHC Safety Study Group report, authoritative CERN background, and peer-reviewed methodological criticism — for which 2 distinct sources were found and kept). No additional 7th/8th candidate cleared the bar: one candidate (an academia.edu-hosted 'Critical Review of Safety Papers Concerning Black Holes at the LHC') was excluded because the page is Cloudflare-challenge-gated (HTTP 403, 'Just a moment...' interstitial) and its peer-review status could not be confirmed; it was not force-fetched or summarized as a substitute. No invented sources were added to reach a round number.
retrieval_method: Direct curl fetch of raw bytes (browser User-Agent, --compressed) per the source-harvest extraction cascade; one source (src-0003) required the Wayback Machine raw-bytes path because the live CERN Document Server (cds.cern.ch) returns an anti-bot proof-of-work challenge page to automated fetches. Extraction via pdftotext -layout (PDFs) or trafilatura (HTML). All 6 verified live and re-audited by an independent agent that re-read every body and re-checked every sha256 against the file on disk.
audit_status: PASS on all 6 sources — independent auditor (separate agent/session from the collector) confirmed each body is genuine full-text prose matching the claimed title/author/venue, not a nav page, paywall stub, JS shell, or AI-written summary; confirmed sha256_original and bytes_original in each source's frontmatter match sha256sum/stat on the corresponding originals/ file.
known_provenance_caveat: src-0006 (Kent 2004) serves body text from the live arXiv record, which is v7 (24 Jul 2015, 'corrections and note added') rather than a scan of the original 2004 print journal issue. Same paper, not a different one; noted in that source's frontmatter as provenance_note.

sources.title: Review of the Safety of LHC Collisions
sources.authors: J. Ellis, G. Giudice, M. L. Mangano, I. Tkachev, U. Wiedemann (LHC Safety Assessment Group)
sources.venue: CERN-PH-TH/2008-136; arXiv:0806.3414; later published, Journal of Physics G: Nuclear and Particle Physics 35 (2008) 115004

sources.role: Key peer-reviewed paper establishing the astrophysical (white dwarf / neutron star) constraint argument
sources.title: Astrophysical implications of hypothetical stable TeV-scale black holes
sources.authors: Steven B. Giddings, Michelangelo L. Mangano

sources.role: Predecessor (2003) LHC Safety Study Group report — the historical baseline the 2008 LSAG report updates
sources.title: Study of Potentially Dangerous Events during Heavy-Ion Collisions at the LHC: Report of the LHC Safety Study Group
sources.authors: J.-P. Blaizot, J. Iliopoulos, J. Madsen, G. G. Ross, P. Sonderegger, H.-J. Specht
sources.retrieval_note: Live cds.cern.ch blocks automated fetches with an Anubis-style proof-of-work anti-bot challenge (served 'Making sure you're not a bot!' HTML instead of the PDF on direct curl). Retrieved instead via the Internet Archive Wayback Machine's raw-bytes path (id_ suffix), snapshot timestamp 20260505221446, confirmed as a genuine PDF (not the challenge page) by file/pdfinfo inspection.

sources.role: Authoritative CERN background — official public explanation of the extra-dimensions / TeV-scale black hole scenario the safety case addresses
sources.title: Extra dimensions, gravitons, and tiny black holes
sources.retrieval_note: Two other candidate CERN FAQ URLs (home.web.cern.ch/.../safety-lhc; home.cern/resources/faqs/will-cern-generate-black-hole) returned HTTP 404 or redirected to a generic landing page — those specific FAQ pages no longer resolve and were not used. cern.ch/lsag/LSAG-Report.pdf (the LSAG group's own mirror) failed DNS resolution (lsag.web.cern.ch) and was not used; src-0001 is served from arXiv instead.

sources.role: Serious peer-reviewed methodological critique of low-probability/high-stakes risk assessment, using the LHC safety case as a worked example
sources.title: Probing the Improbable: Methodological Challenges for Risks with Low Probabilities and High Stakes
sources.authors: Toby Ord, Rafaela Hillerbrand, Anders Sandberg

sources.role: Peer-reviewed critique of the statistical/methodological standards used in collider-safety risk assessments; predates but directly frames the LSAG-era debate
sources.title: A Critical Look at Risk Assessments for Global Catastrophes
sources.retrieval_note: Body served is arXiv v7 (24 Jul 2015 revision), not a scan of the original 2004 print issue. Same paper; see provenance_note in sources/src-0006.md frontmatter.

excluded_candidates.candidate: A Critical Review of Safety Papers Concerning Black Holes at the LHC (academia.edu upload)
excluded_candidates.reason_excluded: Site returned HTTP 403 with a Cloudflare 'Just a moment...' interstitial on direct fetch; peer-review status of the venue could not be confirmed without bypassing the challenge. Not force-fetched via a bot-evasion technique, and not summarized from the search snippet as a substitute for the real text.
HTTP 404 — page no longer exists at this path.
Redirects to a generic LHC landing page, not the specific FAQ answer — treated as dead/reorganized.
DNS resolution failure — host does not resolve.
